content: Mission RFC enforcement via CHECK constraints: MissionState ('active'|'frozen'|'completed'|'failed'|'cancelled') and MissionPhase ('understand'|'align'|'decide'|'plan'|'execute'|'done') are enforced at table creation time in store.ts CREATE TABLE, ensuring database integrity and preventing invalid state transitions at the SQL level.

content: Workstream model: each Workstream maps 1:1 to a tracker task; briefPath points to optional Markdown brief file within artifactRoot; briefRevision (SHA-256) in SpecMeta detects when brief changes; stale specs block lead dispatch until regenerated by analyst. See src/missions/workstreams.ts and src/missions/spec-meta.ts

Execution handoff chain: packageHandoffs filters to (planned|active) workstreams with all deps completed; slingArgsFromHandoff builds 'ov sling {taskId} --capability lead --parent execution-director --files {fileScope} --spec {briefPath}'; execution-director validates briefs exist before dispatch. See src/missions/workstream-control.ts and src/missions/workstreams.ts

File scope ownership: validateWorkstreamsFile enforces non-overlapping fileScope across workstreams to prevent merge conflicts by design; each file can only be claimed by one workstream. Merge queue respects these boundaries. See src/missions/workstreams.ts::validateWorkstreamsFile

content: Mission store schema: single 25-column missions table in sessions.db. Core columns: state (active|frozen|completed|failed|stopped|suspended), phase (understand|align|decide|plan|execute|done), current_node (position as 'phase:state' string), session bindings (analyst/director/coordinator IDs), paused resources (workstream IDs, lead names, reason). Timestamps: created_at, updated_at, started_at, completed_at, first_freeze_at. Freeze gate: pending_user_input (0/1), pending_input_kind (question|approval|decision|clarification|NULL), pending_input_thread_id. Reopen counter incremented on each unfreeze. Idempotent migration v1 handles backward compat (cancelled→stopped, planning→plan, old phase values→execute).

title: Standalone engine model for graph Phase 2
rationale: Avoids modifying DEFAULT_MISSION_GRAPH, keeps lifecycle.ts as authority for main transitions, feature flag naturally gates behavior

content: MissionGraphNode is a discriminated union with kind:'lifecycle' (has phase/state) and kind:'cell' (has cellType, no phase/state). Subgraph internal nodes use CellGraphNode. Cell node IDs prefixed with cellType: for namespace isolation.

Graph engine uses gate:'async' for multi-agent fan-out (not gate:'human'). advanceNode() is on GraphEngine only, not HandlerContext. Plan-review-lead calls ov mission engine advance after collecting verdicts.

engine-wiring.ts bridge module pattern (overstory-d8bb): CELL_REGISTRY maps cellType strings to ReviewCellDefinition objects with buildGraph()/buildHandlers(). Cell graph node IDs must be prefixed with cellType (plan-review:dispatch). Noop handler returns trigger 'default' so handler-driven edges must use trigger 'default'. Gate edges (all-returned) use meaningful trigger names. shouldUseEngine delegates feature flag check to config.mission?.graphExecution. startCellEngine is idempotent via checkpoint resume — createGraphEngine auto-resumes from latest checkpoint when startNodeId is omitted.

content: Quickstart wizard is first interactive CLI command. No interactive libraries exist (zero inquirer/prompts). Use node:readline (Bun built-in) for line input. TTY detection via process.stdin.isTTY === true (strict equality, can be undefined).

name: mission-complete-deadlock
description: ov mission complete deadlocks when coordinator agent is still running — it tries to stop all agents including the coordinator itself. Workaround: stop all other agents first, then have the operator run ov mission complete after the coordinator session ends.

name: Mission: verify-new-features
description:
Objective: VERIFICATION MISSION: Test all features shipped in waves 1-5 of roadmap #37. This is a real task that also serves as integration test.

TASK: Create a verification report at docs/feature-verification.md documenting which new features work.

VERIFICATION CHECKLIST for coordinator and workers:
1. Run 'ov rate-limits' — verify headroom output (src/headroom/)
2. Run 'ov health-policy evaluate --dry-run' — verify policy engine works (src/health/policy/)
3. Run 'ov context generate && ov context show' — verify project analysis (src/context/)
4. Run 'ov next-improvement --all --json' — verify multi-result ranking with estimatedImpact (src/health/recommendations.ts)
5. Run 'ov health --json' — verify quality signals from review/eval sources appear
6. Run 'ov adaptive --json' — verify adaptive parallelism signals (src/adaptive/)
7. Run 'ov compat --help' — verify compat command exists (src/compat/)
8. Run 'ov export --help' — verify export command exists (src/observability/)
9. Run 'ov workflow --help' — verify workflow command exists (src/workflow/)
10. Run 'ov quickstart --help' — verify quickstart command exists (src/quickstart/)
11. Check src/reminders/ module loads — import and verify ReminderSource
12. Check src/artifact-status/ module — verify classify functions exist
13. Run 'ov eval run evals/temporal-smoke' if exists — verify temporal assertions
14. Check mission graph types — verify subgraph/handler extensions exist in src/missions/graph.ts
15. Check src/resilience/ — verify circuit breaker and retry policy types exist

Write results to docs/feature-verification.md with pass/fail for each item. Use actual CLI output as evidence.. Agents: 3 (coordinator-mission, mission-analyst, execution-director). Tokens: 8,530in/73,428out ($31.52). Tokens by role: unknown: 8,168in/48,745out, mission-analyst: 109in/10,977out, coordinator-mission: 176in/9,399out, execution-director: 77in/4,307out. State: completed. Phase: done.

content: Plan review loop: round 1 catches broad issues (wrong npm names, missing capability sets, no bounds checking), round 2 catches architectural issues (init.ts is canonical for manifest, dependency graph gaps), round 3 converges. 3-round review is typical for 4-workstream plans.

Mission resource isolation gaps: worktrees (.overstory/worktrees/{agentName}), branches (overstory/{agentName}/{taskId}), mail (no mission_id column), and tmux sessions (hard-coded global names in roles.ts) are all NOT mission-scoped. Parallel missions require mission-slug prefixing across all these namespaces.

content: getActiveList() returns all active AND frozen missions to support multi-mission concurrent execution. Unlike getActive() (single LIMIT 1), getActiveList() enables resolveActiveMissionContext() ambiguity detection: throw if 2+ missions exist, auto-select if exactly 1.

--mission flag pattern (src/commands/mission.ts:62-73): resolveExplicitMission() resolves flag as id-or-slug (getById first, fallback getBySlug). Applied to 8 mission subcommands (update/answer/handoff/pause/resume/stop/complete/status) requiring explicit mission when 2+ active.

name: getActiveList multi-mission query pattern
description: MissionStore.getActiveList() returns ALL active/frozen missions (ordered by created_at DESC). Distinct from getActive() which returns single most-recent mission (LIMIT 1). Infrastructure ready for multi-mission support: (1) type updates DashboardData/StatusData to accept Mission[], (2) loop rendering in panels, (3) health score aggregation strategy needed.

content: DashboardData.mission: Mission | null and StatusData.mission: Mission | null establish single-mission interface pattern. To support multi-mission: change to missions: Mission[]; affects src/dashboard/data.ts loadDashboardData(), src/commands/status.ts gatherStatus(), all render functions expecting mission argument. Mail system already supports missionId parameter (src/missions/mail-bridge.ts has fallback pattern). Backward compat: keep getActive() method, mark as deprecated in favor of getActiveList().

content: frozen_at column tracks when a mission entered frozen state (not first_freeze_at which is immutable). freeze() sets it, unfreeze() and completeMission() clear it. Used for timeout detection via checkMissionFreezeTimeout().

Concurrent freeze race fix: when mission.state === 'frozen', append new threadId to pending_input_thread_id JSON array via appendMissionThreadId() instead of calling freeze() which would overwrite. First freeze wins for kind/state; subsequent ones only accumulate thread IDs.

dispatch_log table (mission_id, workstream_id PK, dispatched_at) records workstream dispatch events for audit trail. logMissionDispatch() uses INSERT OR IGNORE to prevent duplicates. Populated in missionHandoff() before sending execution_handoff mail.

MISSION_PENDING_SENDERS in src/missions/mail-bridge.ts uses exact-match Set and does not include slug-scoped coordinator names (e.g. coordinator-sse-realtime). When running parallel missions, question-type mail from coordinators fails to trigger freeze on the correct mission.
resolution: Workaround: directly call missionStore.freeze()/unfreeze() via bun -e script. Fix needed: add prefix matching or pass mission ID through mail metadata.

description: coordinator-mission-mgmt not in MISSION_PENDING_SENDERS set, blocking auto-freeze. TDZ bug in workstream-control.ts:552 blocked handoff.
resolution: Freeze workaround: send question mail with --agent coordinator. TDZ fix: move edAgentName declaration before usage (e3111bb).

description: SSE extension as separate critical-path workstream unblocked 4 parallel workstreams. JSON SSE events for notifications decoupled detection from rendering. 5 workstreams with clean file scope separation: 4 Tier 1 merges, 1 Tier 2, 1 Tier 3.

content: Mission phases (understand|align|decide|plan|execute|done) are orthogonal to states (active|frozen|completed|failed|stopped|suspended). Phases represent workflow progression; states represent lifecycle. Freeze tracking uses pendingUserInput bool + pendingInputKind + pendingInputThreadId to hold missions across mail continuations.

Workstreams schema: { id, taskId, objective, fileScope, dependsOn, briefPath, status } is runtime-enforced contract in plan/workstreams.json. validateWorkstreamsFile checks: non-empty fields, circular dependency detection, non-overlapping fileScope across all workstreams. Legacy fields (name, capability, files) must not appear.

ArtifactStaleness tracking uses ARTIFACT_DEPENDENCIES map: brief & spec track [mission.md, plan/workstreams.json]; mission-plan tracks [mission.md, decisions.md, open-questions.md, plan/workstreams.json]; review-output tracks [mission.md, decisions.md]; mission-score tracks [results/summary.json, results/review.json]. Snapshot stored in .artifact-staleness.json using SHA-256 hashing. First run is fresh baseline.

formatTddOverlay dispatcher pattern (overstory-792a): pure function dispatches to capability-specific formatters (formatBuilderTddOverlay, formatReviewerTddOverlay, formatLeadTddOverlay) based on capability string; returns '' for undefined tddMode or unknown capability. Switch exhaustive over TddMode='full'|'light'|'skip'|'refactor'.

startArchitectRole() in roles.ts follows exact startMissionAnalyst() pattern: DI via _deps, storeFactory, tmuxSession scoped with missionSlug prefix 'ov-architect-', binds architectSessionId via bindSessions. Type cast (as unknown as) used for forward-compat when parallel data-layer-builder adds the field to MissionStore.

Flash Quality handoff gate in missionHandoff() (workstream-control.ts): check hasFlashQuality = dispatchableWorkstreams.some(ws => ws.tddMode === 'full' || ws.tddMode === 'light'). CRITICAL: use === 'full' || === 'light', NOT !== 'skip', to avoid matching undefined (legacy missions). Path computed via join(paths.planDir, 'architecture.md') when MissionArtifactPaths.architectureMd not yet available.

name: Mission: flash-quality
description: Objective: Implement Flash Quality TDD-first pipeline per GitHub issue #79. Coordinator: run 'gh issue view 79' to read the full spec — it contains the complete pipeline design, architect agent definition, tester agent definition, architecture.md schema, test-plan schema, mode-dependent overlays, and agent prompt audit with per-agent changes. All 6 comments on the issue contain detailed design decisions. This is a large mission — read the full issue body AND all comments before planning.. Agents: 26 (coordinator-mission, mission-analyst, scout, plan-review-lead, plan-security-critic, plan-performance-critic, plan-simulator, plan-devil-advocate, plan-second-opinion, execution-director, lead, builder, reviewer). Tokens: 90,658in/643,469out ($257.94). Tokens by role: unknown: 45,525in/163,056out, builder: 12,838in/147,487out, lead: 25,127in/92,104out, execution-director: 493in/51,648out, scout: 3,701in/49,195out, mission-analyst: 175in/36,892out, plan-simulator: 844in/20,414out, coordinator-mission: 186in/19,122out, plan-second-opinion: 73in/15,164out, reviewer: 44in/14,905out, plan-devil-advocate: 36in/12,385out, plan-security-critic: 41in/11,655out, plan-performance-critic: 1,575in/9,442out. State: completed. Phase: done.
Mission flash-quality scored 75/100 on coordination-fit: agents: 26, duration: 9434599ms, metrics: 38

name: Workstream decomposition: flash-quality
description: 7 workstreams: flash-types, architecture-parser, test-plan-parser, tdd-overlay, lifecycle-wiring, agent-definitions, agent-prompts. Capabilities: coordinator-mission, mission-analyst, scout, plan-review-lead, plan-security-critic, plan-performance-critic, plan-simulator, plan-devil-advocate, plan-second-opinion, execution-director, lead, builder, reviewer. Dependencies: architecture-parser → flash-types; test-plan-parser → flash-types; tdd-overlay → flash-types; lifecycle-wiring → flash-types, architecture-parser, test-plan-parser, tdd-overlay; agent-prompts → agent-definitions.

name: structured-mulch-integration decomposition
description: 4 workstreams (mulch-client-extensions, audience-filtering, architecture-sync-agent, agent-prompt-updates). 2 parallel tracks: WS1→WS2+WS3, WS4 independent. All merged clean (Tier 1). ~25 agents, ~1h20m wall time.
When coordinator runs ov mission complete, the deferred self-kill (SIGTERM) fires before complete logic finishes, leaving mission in active state.
resolution: Workaround: update mission store directly after SIGTERM. Root fix needed in lifecycle.ts terminalizeMission flow — complete store update must happen before self-kill.

content: holdout integration ordering: holdout check runs after drainAgentInbox loop and before state transition in terminalizeMission(); uses dynamic import('./holdout.ts') for lazy loading; config gated via mission.holdout.enabled \!== false; on failure sets process.exitCode=1, closes store, returns early with nulls
